beats.action: Inside the damp, shadowed war room, Kaelen slides a black obsidian marker into the narrowest point of the pass on a stone map.
beats.camera: Medium shot, slight zoom on Kaelen's hand and the map.

dialogue.text: The sect's vanguard is three hours from the outer gates. Their momentum is absolute. Standard defensive formations will fail within the hour.

beats.action: Lyra leans over the table, her hand resting heavily on the hilt of her sword, knuckles white.
beats.camera: Medium close-up on Lyra's face and hand on sword hilt.

dialogue.text: Then we meet them at the ridge. We draw our steel and hold the line. That is what we are trained for, Kaelen. Honor demands we stand.

beats.action: Kaelen looks directly at Lyra, his expression unchanging.

dialogue.text: Honor will keep you warm in a mass grave. If we engage them directly, we have a zero percent probability of survival. The math does not support your conviction.

beats.action: Master Valerius stands in the deeper shadows of the chamber, hands clasped behind his back, watching the dust settle on the stone map.
beats.camera: Medium shot, slightly low angle, emphasizing his presence in the shadows.

dialogue.text: Speak the alternative, Kaelen. But do not dress it in soft words. We have no time for illusions.

beats.action: Kaelen turns his gaze towards the map, pointing to a specific area with a precise, calculated gesture. Valerius listens intently.
beats.camera: Medium shot, over Valerius's shoulder, focusing on Kaelen and the map.

dialogue.text: We collapse the reservoir above the eastern basin. The resulting torrent will sweep their entire main host into the lower gorge, sealing them in a tomb of rock and water.

beats.action: Lyra frowns, tracing the line of the reservoir down to the basin on the map with her finger, her eyes widening as realization hits her.
beats.camera: Close-up on Lyra's face and hand as she traces the map.

dialogue.text: The third garrison is stationed in that basin. They are holding the secondary bottleneck. You're talking about drowning three hundred of our own men.

beats.action: Kaelen maintains eye contact with Lyra, his face implacable.

dialogue.text: If we warn them to evacuate, the enemy will detect the retreat and pivot. The garrison must remain in place to anchor the enemy's attention. They must fight, and they must die, so the trap can succeed.

beats.action: Lyra recoils slightly, trembling with anger, her voice rising.
beats.camera: Close-up on Lyra's face, slight shaky cam to convey her anger.

dialogue.text: That is betrayal! It is murder! How can you look at a map and trade lives like pebbles on a path?

beats.action: Kaelen stands firm, undeterred by Lyra's outburst.
beats.camera: Medium close-up on Kaelen, emphasizing his stillness against Lyra's agitation.

dialogue.text: Because three hundred lives buy the survival of three thousand. It is a simple equation, Lyra. Your outrage does not alter the arithmetic of war.

beats.action: Lyra turns away from Kaelen, looking pleadingly at Valerius in the gloom, extending a hand slightly.
beats.camera: Medium shot, over Kaelen's shoulder, showing Lyra appealing to Valerius.

dialogue.text: Master Valerius, please. Tell him this is madness. We cannot build our victory on the silent slaughter of our brothers. Command us to ride out instead.

beats.action: Valerius closes his eyes for a moment, then looks down at his worn, scarred hands, bearing the invisible weight of centuries.
beats.camera: Close-up on Valerius's face, then slowly pans down to his hands.

dialogue.text: There are no good paths left to us, Lyra. Only those who die for nothing, and those who die to keep the light burning. Kaelen's logic is cruel... but it is correct.

beats.action: Lyra steps back, her hand falling limply away from her sword hilt. Her face drains of color as the reality of her complicity sinks in.
beats.camera: Medium close-up on Lyra's face and upper body, slight pull back.

dialogue.text: We are no better than the monsters at our gates.

beats.action: Kaelen looks directly at Lyra, his expression firm and unyielding.

dialogue.text: We are survivors. That is the only distinction that matters.

beats.action: Kaelen reaches down and firmly presses the black obsidian marker into the center of the basin on the map, crushing the small wooden token representing the third garrison beneath it.
beats.camera: Close-up on Kaelen's hand pressing the marker, focusing on the crushing of the token.
